zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Sophia van Altena de Montbeliard於1128年出生在荷蘭布拉班特斯登時，她的父親Thierry de Montbéliard III47歲，母親Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg36歲。她於1149年與Willem van Lijnden結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1180年4月28日在荷蘭武特雷特武特雷特享年52歲，埋葬在以色列מחוז ירושליםיְרוּשָׁלַיִם。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Sophia van Altena de Montbeliard于1128年出生在荷兰布拉班特斯登时，她的父亲Thierry de Montbéliard III47岁，母亲Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg36岁。她于1149年与Willem van Lijnden结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1180年4月28日在荷兰武特雷特武特雷特享年52岁，埋葬在以色列מחוז ירושליםיְרוּשָׁלַיִם。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Altena
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch and North German: habitational name from any of several places and houses so named. For a long time the placename has been explained as al te na ‘too close’, referring to its location nearby another object. Possibly this associative explanation has been applied for some Altena places indeed, but in North Brabant the toponym Land of Altena, with the castle of the noblemen of Altena, seems to be adopted from an old river name Huolthena (996), which probably means olten a ‘old a’, a being a general noun for ‘waterway’.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

nameMeanings.name: Sophia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek word meaning ‘wisdom’. The Eastern cult of  St Sophia  arose as a result of misinterpretation of the phrase Hagia Sophia ‘holy wisdom’ as if it meant ‘St Sophia’. The name became popular in England in the 17th and 18th centuries—the heroine of  Fielding's  novel Tom Jones (  1749 ) is called  Sophia Weston—and  has been increasing in popularity since the 1990s. In Scotland it has been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Beathag .

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Sophia van Altena de Montbeliard was born in 1128, in Heusden, Brabant, Netherlands, her father, Thierry de Montbéliard III, was 47 and her mother, Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg, was 36. She married Willem van Lijnden in 1149. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 3 daughters. She died on 28 April 1180, in Utrecht, Utrecht, Netherlands, at the age of 52, and was buried in Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israel.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Sophia van Altena de Montbeliard est née en 1128, à Heusden, Brabant, Pays-Bas, son père, Thierry de Montbéliard III, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Willem van Lijnden en 1149. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 28 avril 1180, à Utrecht, Utrecht, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 52 ans, et a été enterrée à Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israël.

de.lifeSummary: Als Agnes Sophia van Altena de Montbeliard 1128 in Heusden, Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Thierry de Montbéliard III 47 und ihre Mutter Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg 36. Sie hat 1149 Willem van Lijnden geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 28. April 1180 im Alter von 52 Jahren in Utrecht, Utrecht, Niederlande und wurde in Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israel begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Sophia van Altena de Montbeliard è nata il nel 1128 nel Heusden, Brabant, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Thierry de Montbéliard III, aveva 47 anni e sua madre, Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato Willem van Lijnden nel 1149. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 3 figlie. Morì il 28 aprile 1180, nel Utrecht, Utrecht, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 52 anni, e fu sepolta a Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israele.

ja.lifeSummary: Agnes Sophia van Altena de Montbeliardが1128年にオランダブラバントヘウスデンで生まれた時，父Thierry de Montbéliard IIIは47歳，母Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburgは36歳であった。1149年にWillem van Lijndenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1180年4月28日にオランダウトレチトウトレチトで52歳で死去し，イスラエルמחוז ירושליםיְרוּשָׁלַיִםにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Sophia van Altena de Montbeliard(이)가 1128년 네덜란드 브라반트 헤우즈든에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thierry de Montbéliard III(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1149년 Willem van Lijnden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1180년 4월 28일 네덜란드 우트레ch트 우트레ch트에서 52세의 나이로 사망했으며 이스라엘 מחוז ירושלים יְרוּשָׁלַיִם에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Agnes Sophia van Altena de Montbeliard nasceu em 1128, em Heusden, Brabant, Países Baixos, seu pai, Thierry de Montbéliard III, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com Willem van Lijnden em 1149. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 28 de abril de 1180, em Utrecht, Utrecht, Países Baixos, com 52 anos, e foi sepultada em Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israel.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Sophia van Altena de Montbeliard родилась в 1128 г. в местности хеусден, брабант, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Thierry de Montbéliard III было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Willem van Lijnden в 1149 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 3 дочери. Она умерла 28 апреля 1180 г. в местности утречт, утречт, Нидерланды в возрасте 52 лет и была похоронена в местности יְרוּשָׁלַיִם, מחוז ירושלים, Израиль.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Agnes Sophia van Altena de Montbeliard nació en 1128, en Heusden, Brabant, Países Bajos, su papá, Thierry de Montbéliard III, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Gertrud Gräfin von Habsburg, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Willem van Lijnden en 1149. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 3 hijas. Murió el 28 de abril de 1180, en Utrecht, Utrecht, Países Bajos, a la edad de 52 años, y fue sepultada en Jerusalem, Jerusalem, Israel.
